trajectory.description: Observation: Val bal_acc 0.697 but test only 0.496 - huge domain gap. Empty (0.12), squirrel (0.11), bobcat (0.30) are weakest. Analysis: Frozen backbone can't adapt to domain shift. Need fine-tuning + domain alignment. Decision: Unfreeze DINOv2 with low LR, add CORAL loss for domain alignment, use deeper head, stronger augmentation, and test-time augmentation in predict.py.

trajectory.description: Observation: Test bal_acc improved from 0.496→0.540 with fine-tuning+CORAL, but bird dropped from 0.74→0.26 and squirrel from 0.11→0.05. Fine-tuning is causing domain overfitting - model adapts to source domains at expense of generalization. Val bal_acc=0.891 vs test=0.540 shows huge domain gap. Analysis: Need domain adversarial training (DANN) to force domain-invariant features, EMA for stable generalization, and label smoothing to prevent overconfident predictions. Also need lower backbone LR to prevent overfitting. Decision: Add DANN with gradient reversal, EMA (decay=0.999), cosine LR with warmup, label smoothing, and train for 20 epochs with more aggressive augmentation.

trajectory.description: Observation: Test bal_acc improved 0.496→0.540→0.617 with DANN+Coral+EMA. Bird(0.37), bobcat(0.33), squirrel(0.37) still weak. Val 0.84 vs test 0.62 shows massive domain gap. Analysis: DANN helps but model still overfits to source domain styles. Need style randomization (MixStyle) to create domain-robust features, focal loss for class imbalance, and checkpoint ensembling for prediction stability. Decision: Add MixStyle to backbone features, use focal loss, save top-3 checkpoints for ensembling, train 30 epochs with lower backbone LR (2e-6) and higher label smoothing (0.15).

trajectory.description: Observation: Test bal_acc went 0.496→0.540→0.617→0.563. Best was iter 3 (DINOv2+DANN+CORAL+EMA, no MixStyle/focal). Iter 4 with MixStyle+focal hurt (0.563). The existing DINOv2 checkpoints are from iter 4 and scored 0.563. Val-test gap is huge (0.73 vs 0.56), confirming severe domain shift. Analysis: MixStyle and focal loss hurt performance - focal loss biases toward rare training classes at expense of common test classes (opossum dropped 0.54→0.28, empty 0.75→0.15). Need to revert to iter 3 setup and add SWA (Stochastic Weight Averaging) for flatter minima that generalize better across domains. Decision: Retrain with iter 3 setup (DINOv2+DANN+CORAL+EMA, no MixStyle/focal) plus SWA averaging of EMA weights from epoch 10+. Use probability averaging for TTA instead of logit averaging.
